item.title: Ask A Government Teacher: What is happening to America and can it be fixed?
item.description: This will probably be my last piece since I will not be a government teacher this school year. There's a lot of stress that goes into teaching government nowadays so I asked for a transfer. Fair warning, I'm not trying to be unbiased anymore because It's pretty hard for most people not to take a stand when 33% of the population cries actual tears of joy for 51% of the population losing a natural right. So, what in the world is happening to America and can it be fixed? Since I've been doing these, America has changed at a rate that I once joked with a professor would take a century. Ironically, it has gone the other direction from the topic of that conversation. You are living it too, you can decide whether for the better or worse. However, in my opinion, if you choose to read it, it is going in a direction that's getting difficult to be optimistic about. I'll preface this point by saying that the Founding Fathers were classically educated. Most of them spoke more than one language and some spoke as many as 5 and two of the languages were Greek & Latin. They grew up with stories from ancient Greece and Rome that celebrated desirable traits in a society and its citizenry. George Washington styled himself after a Roman named Cincinnatus (great story if you haven't read it). Hamilton, Jay and Madison called themselves 'Publius' in the Federalist Papers and the fasces (eponymously affiliated with the opposite of a democracy) has always been all over American government. The Founders wanted to make a more perfect form of ancient Athens and Rome and they designed it to be flexible and adaptive to the needs of the people, it's why we call the Constitution "a living document". It isn't anymore. American government has ground to a halt, wracked with corruption and infighting. Worse, there's no one person or problem to solve either and getting people together to solve them would be practically impossible at this point for several key reasons. Many political historians believe the 2010 Citizens United vs FEC SCOTUS decision opened the door to utterly absurd amounts of money going towards political campaigns and though no one wants to admit it, politicians. Further compounding the situation is the legality of gerrymandering, especially in population centers, increasingly restrictive voting laws in the 2020 election battleground states AND, if all that fails, politicians just calling the people's vote illegitimate and using mega news conglomerates to convince people into believing that their country can only be saved through violence. This will ensure that the popular vote, that keeps the people feeling validated and engaged, will continue to be marginalized. But that's not all, folks... Politicians and judges are emboldened by the absence of term limits to shore up growing blocks of like-minded colleagues in the Senate, House of Representatives and the most relevant, the Supreme Court of the United States, a traditionally apolitical branch of the federal government. Oh, and in case you're curious, SCOTUS Justices are in there for life (unless they step down or are impeached which last happened the same year Alexander Hamilton died). I can't emphasize enough how critically unfortunate it is that the SCOTUS is not only making decisions along party lines but that their majority opinions seem to be worded in ways that seem like insults to the desires of the vast majority of the American people. Two decisions blurred the lines between church and state, which the Founders were very serious about creating a government totally free of religious influence. One decision that ruined hopes of any kind of meaningful gun control which, again, the VAST majority of Americans are calling for. Worst of all, they overturned a 50 year precedent that had given women a MAJOR stepping stone towards equality with their male counterparts. The overturning of Roe v Wade to allow states to decide a basic human right is a devastating reversal for a country who fought its deadliest war over the states right to decide human rights. This was all just the past month. Unfortunately, the three Justices who gave the SCOTUS the power to make these rulings are all relatively young so it's not going to stop anytime soon. This is all exacerbated by the fact that there is no limit whatsoever on what the media can broadcast in terms of accuracy. I'll clarify, technically there ARE limits but the media corporations have the money and armies of lawyers to tie up courts for years until the aggrieved party gives up. Look up the Fox News vs Dominion case currently grinding through the courts. Another conservative SCOTUS ruled in the late 80's that the Fairness Doctrine, a 4 decade policy that enforced consequences via the FCC for networks broadcasting biased and/or ultimately false perspectives, was a violation of the Freedom of Press. As a result, the media has been constantly pushing the boundaries of what is factual to the point that certain networks have gaslighted people into believing that the goals of one party in particular are nothing less that pure "socialism" "communism" and as of April officially "evil". That's is the worst part; they have made us hate each other. We've all seen the memes, read the articles, discussed it with others, had it out with relatives at Thanksgiving; the worst thing about all of this was not how Trump violently and repeatedly abused our democracy and his long list of outrages. He had an extensive history of wrongdoings and dubious behavior BEFORE he became president. It was how effectively he and his cronies turned us against each other. By electing him, we gave him the validation to show that the worst parts of humanity could be openly displayed without consequence. Families have been split apart, friendships destroyed, professional opportunities lost because of conversations weren't about trivial issues like a president's ability to use proper vocabulary or about extramarital affairs but about critical issues like fighting racism and protecting human rights. He brought out the worst and made us hate each other. Now, even though he continues to quietly rant and rave in Mar A Lago, the decisions Trump made (e.g. the decisions of the SCOTUS he built) are eroding Americans faith in our government while simultaneously broadcasting that the other side are a group that are too far gone from any chance of redemption. Personally, I am afraid of where this is heading now. When people feel the government won't protect them, they get desperate and desperation is wildly unpredictable and dangerous. Is there a peaceful way out of this? Yes, but the already slim chance is slipping away the closer we get to election day. *What I mean by that is the Jan 6th Committee will likely be dissolved if, and all polls are predicting it, the GOP takes back both Houses in November. There's a standard in functioning democracies that when leaders who have openly and conclusively crossed the line, they step down from their position of power or are held responsible. Why is this relevant? Unless the Rule of Law is upheld and American politicians face real consequences for their actions there will be no end to the culture wars that are exacerbating our divisions. Historically, there is only one other way this ends and I'm not going to say it, that's how scary it is. As I said, it's not an uplifting read. I believe we're at a defining crossroads in our history that rivals 1861. The best thing YOU can do is get out and vote and encourage everyone you know to do so as well because two more years of an impotent president with zero legislative support is a dark prospect. As always, keep calm and We The People. -Your local government teacher
item.dc:creator: Republicrat

item.title: This isn't just some backwoods church meeting. It's a nat'l conference convened by the Family Research Council, far right lobbyists that regularly meet w the highest Republicans in govt.

item.title: F Fox News.
item.description: The young girl was raped in early May and police in Columbus were first alerted of her pregnancy on June 22, according to police documents and court testimony. A little more than a week later, the girl underwent an abortion in Indiana. Dr. Caitlin Bernard, an Indianapolis-based Ob-Gyn, reported the abortion procedure to the Indiana Department of Health on July 2, three days after the abortion was performed, as required by the department, according to documents obtained from the agency by CNN.
item.dc:creator: DraMoonBreaker

item.title: Apparently even the rock bottom has a basement.
item.description: Edit Source : https://thehill.com/homenews/senate/3559360-gop-senator-blocks-bill-to-protect-interstate-travel-for-abortion/ Alternative : https://www.cnn.com/2022/07/14/politics/republicans-block-senate-bill-abortion-travel-states/index.html
item.dc:creator: DraMoonBreaker

item.title: Butter Chicken Curry with Potatoes
item.description: This is a recipe I came up with because my wife and I weren't fans of the canned, jarred, or concentrated Butter Curry sauces. I normally use a crock pot, but a stock pot would work too. Here's my recipe: 2 lg. cans tomatoes 1 Small Onion 1 lg. can chicken stock 1 can evaporated milk or heavy cream 2 sticks butter 1 Lb. Chicken (cubed) 1/2 Lb. Potatoes (cubed) These need to be measured with the heart: Honey Tomato Paste Minced garlic Black pepper Onion powder Cumin Turmeric Coriander Garam Masala Ginger Chili powder Salt Optional; Beef, Goat, or Lamb instead of chicken -Sauté 1/2 chopped onion in butter and garlic in frying pan. -Add Chicken to pan. Season with cumin, tumeric, coriander, salt, and pepper. Fry until seared. -Remove chicken and place in crock pot, cook potatoes in pan until tender. -Mince tomatoes, 1/2 onion, and garlic, in food processor. -Put minced vegitables, chicken, and potatoes into crock pot, fill 1 tomato can with chicken stock and add it to the curry. (1:2 Ratio) -Stir in all spices, butter, cream, tomato paste, and honey. -You may need to add more spices as the curry cooks. -Simmer for at least 4 hours until desired thickness. Serve over Basmati Rice with a side of toasted naan bread. I'm no chef, but this is how I make it and it comes out great every time! Thanks for letting me share. ☺️ Most Viral Edit: Holy crap! I didn't think you guys would like my recipe that much! I truly appreciate the tips to try to make it better, I'll do my best to try them the next time I make this. :) I should also say, that when I make naan bread, I also make my own garlic butter. I slather it on top before toasting in the oven, and it elevates it to a whole new level! When I cook the basmati rice I tend to add chives to the rice cooker, so they can cook with the rice. I'm not sure if it helps or not... Thank you all for the accolades and the updoots!
item.dc:creator: GusTheBard
